evidence: buildIndexEntry returns domain, latest_week, critical_count, serious_count, pages_scanned, snapshot_url; writeApiFiles writes index.json with schema_version and domains array — verified by api-writer.test.js (tests/unit/api-writer.test.js)
buildSnapshot returns findings ledger, weekly series, inventory summary, tech_findings, and summary block — verified by buildSnapshot tests in tests/unit/api-writer.test.js
buildWeekFindings maps bugs to FindingEntry with rule_id, severity, pages_affected, wcag_sc, wcag_level, trend_status — verified by buildWeekFindings tests in tests/unit/api-writer.test.js
writeApiFiles called from src/aggregate.js after the domain loop (commit 10ad1ae3); no separate server or deployment step added
All three file types include schema_version: '1'; verified by writeApiFiles test 'all generated files have schema_version 1' in tests/unit/api-writer.test.js
writeApiFiles writes to path.join(docsDir, 'api', 'v1', ...); snapshot_url and findings_url in buildIndexEntry use /api/v1/ prefix — verified by URL path tests in tests/unit/api-writer.test.js

buildIndexEntry includes domain, latest_week, critical_count, serious_count, pages_scanned, snapshot_url, findings_url; writeApiFiles writes { schema_version, domains } to index.json — verified by buildIndexEntry and writeApiFiles tests
writeApiFiles(DIRS.docs, ...) wired into src/aggregate.js at line 341 (commit 10ad1ae3); DIRS.docs is the same docs/ directory used for existing HTML and data output
All JSON.stringify calls in writeApiFiles use JSON.stringify(data, null, 1) indent; verified in src/lib/api-writer.js writeApiFiles function (commit 251cd88f)

snapshot.json excludes the per-URL pages inventory array (asserted in buildSnapshot test 'does not include top-level pages array'); weekly findings are one week only; index.json has one entry per domain — no file approaches 5 MB for any known target
npm run test:unit: 107 pass (previously 91 passing + 16 new from api-writer); 2 pre-existing failures in prune.test.js are unrelated to this feature and existed before WP01
writeApiFiles writes sequential small JSON files using Node fs.writeFileSync; no network I/O, no heavy computation; build time impact is negligible (<1% expected)
src/lib/api-writer.js imports only node:fs and node:path (both Node built-ins); no entries added to package.json dependencies (commit 251cd88f)
All output is fs.writeFileSync to docs/api/v1/**; no server, no dynamic routing, no fetch/network calls in api-writer.js
src/lib/api-writer.js uses only node:fs and node:path; wired into existing src/aggregate.js — no new build tooling added
docs/ is gitignored locally (confirmed in .gitignore); API files under docs/api/v1/ follow the same gitignore pattern and will be deployed as part of the GitHub Pages artifact
VA domains are excluded by the existing hf_only guard in scan.yml before aggregate runs; api-writer receives only the domains aggregate processes — no VA-specific logic needed
